decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly getting powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Griselbrand into play using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It also utilizes cards like Lotus Petal and City of Traitors to generate mana quickly. Atraxa, Grand Unifier can help protect the combo pieces from removal spells. The deck also includes disruption cards like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo. Overall, the goal is to cheat big creatures into play early and overpower the opponent before they can react.

The main concept of this combo Painter deck is to assemble the Painter's Servant and Grindstone combo to mill the opponent's entire deck, while utilizing artifacts like Urza's Saga, Karn, the Great Creator, and Mishra's Research Desk for additional synergies and utility. Using efficient mana acceleration with cards like Grim Monolith and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to control the game with disruptive cards like Kozilek's Command and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with a powerful win condition.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble an infect combo by playing creatures such as Blighted Agent and Glistener Elf while using cards like Berserk and Invigorate to pump them up and deal 10 infect damage to the opponent. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect your creatures and disruption cards like Stifle to slow down the opponent's game plan. The deck utilizes mana acceleration from cards like Noble Hierarch and cantrips like Brainstorm to find the necessary pieces for the combo.

The deck is built around the concept of using a high number of spells to quickly and efficiently combo off and win the game. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Simian Spirit Guide, the deck aims to cast a large number of spells in a single turn to either mill cards from the opponent's library or generate a win condition with Thassa's Oracle. The deck also includes protection in the form of Pact of Negation and disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to help ensure the combo goes off smoothly.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like City of Traitors and Ancient Tomb, and then disrupt opponents' strategies with aggressive creatures like Hexing Squelcher and Broadside Bombardiers. The deck also utilizes artifacts like Chalice of the Void and The One Ring to control the board and protect its own creatures. Sundering Eruption and other removal spells help deal with threats, while cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provide additional utility. This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with fast, powerful creatures and disruptive plays.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize aggressive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler, Murktide Regent, Delver of Secrets, and Orcish Bowmasters to quickly pressure the opponent while disrupting their game plan with cards like Force of Will, Daze, and Wasteland. The deck also includes efficient spells like Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat for removal, as well as card selection and manipulation with cards like Mishra's Bauble, Ponder, and Brainstorm. The mana base consists of dual lands to support the Grixis color combination, allowing for flexibility in casting spells across different colors.

The main concept of this Control-Stoneblade deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan while efficiently and effectively setting up your own win conditions. Utilizing a combination of removal spells, countermagic, and card advantage engines like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind them out. The Stoneforge Mystic package provides versatile threats in Batterskull, Kaldra Compleat, and Cryptic Coat, while the inclusion of Dress Down and Flame of Anor offers additional disruption and control options. Thundertrap Trainer serves as a finisher to close out the game.

The deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly through cards like Dark Ritual and Lion's Eye Diamond in order to cast a powerful combo spell like Tendrils of Agony or Ad Nauseam to win the game in a single turn. It also includes cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own strategy. The deck utilizes the graveyard with cards like Necrodominance and Underground Mortuary to maximize its resources and has access to a toolbox of cards through Wishclaw Talisman and Beseech the Mirror.

The deck focuses on utilizing Gaea's Cradle to generate massive amounts of mana and ramp into powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth and Wight of the Reliquary. It also includes cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order to tutor for key creatures, while also using control elements like Wasteland and Collector Ouphe to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to establish board control and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful creatures and mana advantage.

The key concept of this deck is to maintain consistent pressure on the opponent through a combination of disruptive spellcasting, powerful creatures, and land destruction. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Fatal Push to control the board and handle threats, while deploying efficient threats like Nethergoyf and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, this deck aims to outpace the opponent and disrupt their game plan. With a focus on tempo and aggression, the Dimir Tempo deck looks to quickly close out games while staying ahead on resources.

The main concept of this deck is to control the tempo of the game by utilizing disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push while also maintaining a strong board presence with efficient creatures like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent. The deck aims to pressure the opponent with aggressive plays while also disrupting their game plan, making it difficult for them to establish a strong board presence. The inclusion of Wasteland adds another layer of disruption, allowing the deck to punish greedy mana bases. The goal is to maintain control of the game and close out quickly with powerful threats.

The deck revolves around quickly assembling a combo to win the game by milling your entire library and casting Thassa's Oracle to win the game on the spot. The deck is designed to be extremely fast and efficient at executing its game plan, utilizing a combination of mana acceleration, discard spells, and combo pieces to consistently win on turn one or two. By utilizing various tutors and fast mana, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to react, making it a high-risk, high-reward strategy in Legacy 1 vs 1 gameplay.

This combo deck aims to cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield quickly using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The deck includes a lot of card draw and protection spells like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to help find and protect the combo pieces. The strategy revolves around setting up the perfect opportunity to cast Show and Tell or Sneak Attack, allowing you to quickly overwhelm your opponent and win the game before they have a chance to react.

The deck focuses on quickly cycling through its spells to ultimately win the game in one explosive turn. By sacrificing creatures with Cabal Therapy and using cards like Dark Ritual and Simian Spirit Guide for fast mana, the deck aims to cast a series of combo pieces like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to mill the entire deck. With Thassa's Oracle as the final piece, the player will win the game by having no cards left in their library when they enter the battlefield. The deck has backup plans with Pact of Negation and reanimation spells for protection and resilience.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up to play powerful Eldrazi creatures like Ulamog and Emrakul using Cloudpost and other lands as mana sources. It utilizes cards like Crop Rotation and Ancient Stirrings to find key lands and artifacts, while also using Karn, the Great Creator to lock down opponents' threats. The deck can disrupt opponents with cards like Karakas and Bojuka Bog, and has alternate win conditions with cards like Planar Nexus. The key strategy is to quickly assemble mana resources and overwhelm opponents with big threats.

The deck focuses on cheating powerful creatures into play from the graveyard quickly, using cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Archon of Cruelty to overwhelm the opponent. It also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Daze to control the game, while leveraging cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brainstorm for card advantage and selection. The mana base is streamlined for consistency, with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure victory.
